name: Accounts & Mailboxes
theme: First real commands — list accounts and mailboxes with full output formatting.

criteria.description: 'mailctl accounts list' command exists and runs without error when given mocked AppleScript output representing two accounts
testSteps: Run 'mailctl accounts list' via CliRunner with mock_osascript returning canned output for two accounts
Verify exit code is 0
Verify output contains both account names
criteria.threshold: Command succeeds with exit code 0 and both accounts visible in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl accounts list' generates AppleScript that queries Mail.app for all accounts, retrieving name, email address(es), account type, and enabled state
testSteps: Run 'mailctl accounts list' with mock_osascript
Inspect the AppleScript string passed to osascript
Verify it queries 'every account' or equivalent Mail.app objects
Verify it retrieves name, email addresses, account type, and enabled state
criteria.threshold: AppleScript queries all four account properties

criteria.description: 'mailctl accounts list' displays a Rich table with columns for: account name, email address(es), account type (IMAP/Exchange/iCloud/POP), and enabled/disabled status
testSteps: Run 'mailctl accounts list' via CliRunner with mocked account data
Verify output contains the account name, email, type, and enabled state for each account
Verify the output is formatted as a table (contains column separators or alignment)
criteria.threshold: All four columns present in table output with correct data

criteria.description: 'mailctl accounts list --json' outputs valid JSON to stdout containing an array of account objects with name, email, type, and enabled fields
testSteps: Run 'mailctl accounts list --json' via CliRunner with mocked data
Parse stdout as JSON
Verify it is a list of objects
Verify each object has name, email/emails, type, and enabled fields
criteria.threshold: Valid JSON array with all required fields per account

criteria.description: 'mailctl accounts list' shows a graceful error message when Mail.app is not running and exits with non-zero code
testSteps: Run 'mailctl accounts list' with mock_osascript configured to raise MailNotRunningError
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify stderr contains a user-friendly error message mentioning Mail.app
criteria.threshold: Non-zero exit code and helpful error message on stderr

criteria.description: 'mailctl accounts list' batches the account query into a single osascript call (not one per account)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl accounts list' with mock_osascript returning data for multiple accounts
Inspect mock_osascript.calls to count how many osascript invocations were made
Verify exactly one osascript call was made
criteria.threshold: Exactly one osascript call for the entire accounts list

criteria.description: 'mailctl mailboxes list' command exists and runs without error when given mocked AppleScript output representing mailboxes across accounts
testSteps: Run 'mailctl mailboxes list' via CliRunner with mock_osascript returning canned mailbox data
Verify exit code is 0
Verify output contains mailbox names
criteria.threshold: Command succeeds with exit code 0 and mailbox data visible

criteria.description: 'mailctl mailboxes list' displays mailbox name, unread count, and total message count in a Rich table
testSteps: Run 'mailctl mailboxes list' with mocked data including mailbox name, unread count, and message count
Verify all three data points are visible in the output for each mailbox
criteria.threshold: Table shows name, unread count, and message count per mailbox

criteria.description: 'mailctl mailboxes list --account <name>' filters mailboxes to only the specified account
testSteps: Run 'mailctl mailboxes list --account Work' with mock returning mailboxes for Work and Personal accounts
Verify only Work account mailboxes appear in output
Verify Personal account mailboxes are absent
criteria.threshold: Only the targeted account's mailboxes appear

criteria.description: 'mailctl mailboxes list' without --account lists mailboxes from all accounts, with account context visible per mailbox
testSteps: Run 'mailctl mailboxes list' with mock returning mailboxes from two accounts
Verify mailboxes from both accounts appear in the output
Verify each mailbox row identifies which account it belongs to
criteria.threshold: All accounts' mailboxes listed with account attribution

criteria.description: 'mailctl mailboxes list --json' outputs valid JSON containing mailbox objects with name, account, unread_count, and message_count fields
testSteps: Run 'mailctl mailboxes list --json' with mocked data
Parse stdout as JSON
Verify it is a list of objects with name, account, unread_count, and message_count
criteria.threshold: Valid JSON array with all required fields per mailbox

criteria.description: 'mailctl mailboxes list' batches AppleScript to fetch all mailbox data in one osascript call per account (not one call per mailbox)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl mailboxes list' with mock returning data for two accounts with multiple mailboxes each
Count the number of osascript calls via mock_osascript.calls
Verify the number of calls is at most the number of accounts (or fewer if batched into a single call)
criteria.threshold: At most one osascript call per account, not per mailbox

criteria.description: 'mailctl mailboxes list --account NonExistent' produces a clear error when the specified account does not exist
testSteps: Run 'mailctl mailboxes list --account NonExistent' with mock returning account data that does not include 'NonExistent'
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify error message mentions the account was not found
criteria.threshold: Non-zero exit code and error message naming the unknown account

criteria.description: A shared output formatting module/function exists that renders data as either a Rich table or JSON based on the --json flag, usable by any command
testSteps: Inspect the source code for a reusable output module or function
Verify both accounts and mailboxes commands delegate to the same output formatting code
Verify it supports both Rich table and JSON modes
criteria.threshold: Shared output code used by both commands, supporting table and JSON

criteria.description: The --json flag is a global option (on the main Typer callback) that works on both 'accounts list' and 'mailboxes list', producing valid JSON to stdout
testSteps: Verify --json is defined as a global option on the Typer app callback
Run 'mailctl accounts list --json' and verify valid JSON output
Run 'mailctl mailboxes list --json' and verify valid JSON output
criteria.threshold: --json works globally across both commands

criteria.description: The --no-color flag disables Rich colour/style output, and TTY detection automatically disables colour when stdout is not a terminal
testSteps: Verify --no-color flag exists on the CLI
Verify the Rich Console is configured with force_terminal or no_color based on the flag
Verify TTY detection logic exists (e.g. Console auto-detection or explicit isatty check)
criteria.threshold: --no-color flag works and TTY detection is implemented

criteria.description: Rich table output uses clean formatting: column headers, appropriate alignment, and truncation/ellipsis for long field values rather than wrapping
testSteps: Read the table formatting code
Verify tables have named column headers
Verify long field values are truncated with ellipsis (max_width, overflow, or no_wrap settings on columns)
criteria.threshold: Tables have headers, alignment, and truncation handling

criteria.description: Error messages go to stderr and data output goes to stdout — verifiable by capturing streams separately in tests
testSteps: Run a command that succeeds and verify data is on stdout (not stderr)
Run a command that fails and verify error message is on stderr (not stdout)
Confirm clean separation: success outputs nothing to stderr, errors output nothing meaningful to stdout
criteria.threshold: Stdout for data, stderr for errors, with clean separation

criteria.description: Exit codes follow convention: 0 for success, 1 for general errors (Mail.app not running, permission denied), 2 for usage errors (bad arguments)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl accounts list' successfully and verify exit code 0
Run 'mailctl accounts list' with Mail.app error and verify exit code 1
Run a command with invalid arguments and verify exit code 2 (or Typer's default for bad usage)
criteria.threshold: Exit code 0 on success, non-zero on failure

criteria.description: Unit tests exist for 'accounts list' covering: success with multiple accounts, success with single account, --json output, Mail.app not running error, and single osascript call assertion
testSteps: Run pytest for the accounts test module
Verify tests exist for: multi-account success, single account, --json, error handling, batch call count
Verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: At least 5 distinct test cases for accounts, all passing

criteria.description: Unit tests exist for 'mailboxes list' covering: success with multiple accounts, --account filter, --json output, account not found error, and batch osascript call assertion
testSteps: Run pytest for the mailboxes test module
Verify tests exist for: multi-account mailboxes, account filter, --json, error handling, batch call count
Verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: At least 5 distinct test cases for mailboxes, all passing

criteria.description: Full test suite (pytest from project root) passes with zero failures, including all Sprint 1 and Sprint 2 tests
testSteps: Run 'pytest -v' from the project root
Verify all tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors)
Verify Sprint 1 engine tests still pass (regression check)
criteria.threshold: All tests pass, exit code 0

criteria.description: 'mailctl accounts --help' and 'mailctl mailboxes --help' produce useful help text, and 'mailctl accounts list --help' describes the command's purpose and available options
testSteps: Run 'mailctl accounts --help' and verify it mentions account-related commands
Run 'mailctl accounts list --help' and verify it describes the list command and its options (--json)
Run 'mailctl mailboxes list --help' and verify it describes the command and the --account option
criteria.threshold: All help outputs are informative and mention key options

criteria.description: Accounts and mailboxes commands are in separate modules (not all in cli.py) with a clean separation between command handlers, AppleScript generation, and output formatting
testSteps: Verify accounts command logic is in its own module (e.g., src/mailctl/accounts.py or src/mailctl/commands/accounts.py)
Verify mailboxes command logic is in its own module
Verify AppleScript generation for accounts/mailboxes is not inline in the command handler — it either uses a helper function or the engine module
criteria.threshold: Commands in separate modules with clean layering

criteria.description: 'mailctl --version' still prints the version string and exits 0, and 'mailctl --help' still lists command groups including accounts and mailboxes
testSteps: Run 'mailctl --version' and verify it prints version and exits 0
Run 'mailctl --help' and verify it lists accounts and mailboxes as available command groups
criteria.threshold: Sprint 1 CLI skeleton still works, with new command groups visible

outOfScope: Nested/hierarchical mailbox path display (nice-to-have but not required — flat name is sufficient for this sprint)
